channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: People who visit adult or gambling websites double their risk of malware
item.description: Visiting adult and gambling websites doubles the risk of inadvertently installing malware onto work devices, according to a new study. Fabio Massacci at the University of Trento and Vrije University in Amsterdam presented the findings of a massive analysis of real-life data from around the world.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Severance Season 2 Sneak Peek: What to Expect | TechRadar
item.description: Apple TV+ drops a Severance season 2 sneak peek, teasing fans with the first eight minutes of the new season. Here's what we can expect from the upcoming episodes and where you can watch them.
item.dc:creator: amelia.schwanke@futurenet.com (Amelia Schwanke), TechRadar

item.title: The Special Olympics embarks on digital journey to empower its athletes
item.description: The Special Olympics is overhauling its technology stack as part of a digital transformation designed to bolster the training of its athletes across the globe.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Business Leaders Prepare for Next Big Outage Amid Growing Cyber Threats
item.description: Research shows 9 in 10 leaders expect another major incident within the next year, with most prioritizing security over readiness
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Cómo trabaja Ikea con la IA: 30.000 empleados formados
item.description: Como la mayoría de las empresas, Ikea se centra en los datos y la inteligencia artificial (IA) para cumplir sus ambiciones de futuro. Por ello, hace cuatro años, la empresa desarrolló una estrategia de datos para todo el grupo, que se ha seguido desde entonces.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: New 2025 laws hit hot topics from AI in movies to rapid-fire guns
item.description: New laws taking effect with the new year will affect Hollywood actors, social media stars and chatty motorists.
item.dc:creator: David A. Lieb, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New European retail gig work app trend may influence US market
item.description: Stores such as Urban Outfitters, Lush and Uniqlo are recruiting gig workers in the U.K. to bulk up their stores.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Cyberattack hits Japan Airlines, delaying flights for holiday travelers
item.description: Japan Airlines said it was hit by a cyberattack Thursday, causing delays to more than 20 domestic flights but the carrier said it was able to stop the onslaught and restore its systems hours later. There was no impact on flight safety, it said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Cybersecurity Trends 2024: A Year Defined by Intrusions and Ransomware Attacks
item.description: A review of the top cybersecurity trends in 2024, including major intrusions into U.S. critical infrastructure and ransomware attacks on the health care sector.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Nikki Haley rips Ramaswamy: 'Nothing wrong' with American culture
item.description: Former United Nations Ambassador Nikki Haley slammed her onetime GOP presidential primary opponent Vivek Ramaswamy on Thursday for arguing American culture is to blame for a lack of U.S.-born engineers.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Leveraging Avaya Experience Platform to accelerate your digital banking transformation
item.description: Banks are striving for digital innovation but regulatory constraints, data security and privacy concerns, integration challenges, and the high costs of enabling change prevent 70% from achieving their transformation goals.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Japan Airlines was hit by a cyberattack, delaying flights during the year-end holiday season
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan Airlines said it was hit by a cyberattack Thursday, causing delays to more than 20 domestic flights but the carrier said it was able to stop the onslaught and restore its systems hours later. There was no impact on flight safety, it said.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fake AI-generated reviews are all over the internet, research shows
item.description: The emergence of generative artificial intelligence tools that allow people to efficiently produce novel and detailed online reviews with almost no work has put merchants, service providers and consumers in uncharted territory, watchdog groups and researchers say.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Banning Chinese-made drones could hurt some Americans
item.description: Russell Hedrick, a North Carolina farmer, flies drones to spray fertilizers on his corn, soybean and wheat fields at a fraction of what it would cost him to use a conventional ground spreader.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: We used Google’s AI to analyze 188 predictions of what’s in store for tech in 2025
item.description: At this time of year investment banks, advertising agencies, and seemingly every other business on the planet share their predictions on what is likely to unfold in the next 12 months. Journalists’ inboxes sag under the weight of unsolicited predictions for the year ahead.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: 5 network automation startups to watch
item.description: To meet digital transformation initiatives, businesses across the economy are seeking ways to automate network operations. According to a report from Mordor Intelligence, the network automation market should top $25 billion by the end of 2024, expanding to more than $71 billion in 2030.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Chris Christie predicts Elon Musk's influence over Trump will end
item.description: Former New Jersey Gov. Chris Christie (R) predicted in a Sunday interview that tech billionaire Elon Musk’s influence over President-elect Trump will end. “When does this end?” ABC News’s Jonathan Karl asked Christie on “This Week” of Musk’s influence on Trump.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: NASA's Parker Solar Probe aims to fly closer to the sun like never before
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A NASA spacecraft aims to fly closer to the sun than any object sent before. The Parker Solar Probe was launched in 2018 to get a close-up look at the sun. Since then, it has flown straight through the sun's corona: the outer atmosphere visible during a total solar eclipse.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Albania to close TikTok for a year blaming it for promoting violence among children
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — Albania's prime minister said Saturday the government will shut down the video service TikTok for one year, blaming it for inciting violence and bullying, especially among children.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Arm’s high-stakes licensing suit against Qualcomm ends in mistrial, but Qualcomm prevails in key areas
item.description: Arm has lost a battle over licensing of its microprocessor designs to Qualcomm, ending doubt over the immediate future of some of the chip maker’s products.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
